tools.name: find_tools
tools.description: Software recommendations backed by measured AI answer data: find the best software/tools for a category r job, ranked by how often AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity) actually recommend them in real buyer-style queries — not by ads or affiliate placement. Use when asked "what softwa

tools.name: get_ai_index
tools.description: AI visibility check — which software AI recommends for a category. Returns the full AI Recommendation Index for one software category: the compplete measured ranking of products AI assistants (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity) recommend, with recommendation share %, average answer position, per-en

tools.name: get_facts
tools.description: Canonical software product facts with sources — pricing, features, integrations, platform, and limits, where every fact carries a source URL and a last-verified date. Use to verify software claims (e.g. current pricing) or to gather grounded data before recommending or comparing tools. Accepts a pro

tools.name: compare
tools.description: Compare software/tools side by side — a fact-by-fact comparison of two products (pricing, features, integrations, limits) with source URLs and verified dates for every claim. Use for "X vs Y" software comparison questions. Accepts product names or domains; pair order does not matter.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_facts · Public sink: find_tools, get_ai_index.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_facts · Public sink: find_tools, get_ai_index

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
